entries.definition:
خ/ط/ط kh–ṭ–ṭ furrows, roads; lines, pen markings, writing, stripes; living quarters; affair, plan, intention; to write. Of this root, خَطَّ takhuṭṭ occurs once in the Qur’an.
تَخُطُّ takhuṭṭ [2nd pers. imperf. of v. خَطَّ khaṭṭa, trans.] to write down, to transcribe (29:48) وَمَا كُنْتَ تَتْلُو مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مِنْ كِتَابٍ وَلَا تَخُطُّهُ بِيَمِينِكَ and you never recited, before it, any book; nor did you write one down with your own right hand. [Had you done so], those who are bent on denial might [then] have had [cause] to doubt.
entries.definition_english:
خ/ط/ط kh–ṭ–ṭ furrows, roads; lines, pen markings, writing, stripes; living quarters; affair, plan, intention; to write. Of this root, خَطَّ takhuṭṭ occurs once in the Qur’an.
تَخُطُّ takhuṭṭ [2nd pers. imperf. of v. خَطَّ khaṭṭa, trans.] to write down, to transcribe (29:48) وَمَا كُنْتَ تَتْلُو مِنْ قَبْلِهِ مِنْ كِتَابٍ وَلَا تَخُطُّهُ بِيَمِينِكَ and you never recited, before it, any book; nor did you write one down with your own right hand. [Had you done so], those who are bent on denial might [then] have had [cause] to doubt.
